shortDescription: USCIS · EB categories · Family preference · Multi-year process

policyAlert.summary: Confirm current USCIS filing fees (I-140, I-485, etc.) on uscis.gov. Green card timelines vary widely by category and country of birth.

alert.title: Category and priority date matter
alert.body: Employment-based (EB) and family-based categories have different wait times. Indian-born applicants often face long backlogs in EB-2/EB-3—set realistic expectations.

about.label: Description
about.value: Lawful permanent residence (green card) via employment sponsorship (EB-1/2/3/5) or family petition (I-130). Multi-step process involving PERM, I-140, priority date, and I-485 or consular processing.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Qualifying job offer/sponsor (EB) or qualifying family relationship · PERM for most EB-2/3 · Priority date current per Visa Bulletin · Medical and character clearances

about.label: India backlog reality
about.value: Indian-born EB-2/EB-3 applicants often wait years after I-140 approval for priority date to become current. Monitor Visa Bulletin monthly.

about.label: Key authority
about.value: USCIS · Department of Labor (PERM) · Department of State (Visa Bulletin/NVC)

about.label: After approval
about.value: Receive green card; maintain residence; eligible for citizenship after qualifying period (typically 5 years).

eligibility.criterion: Qualifying EB category or family relationship

redFlagsBanner: If priority date not current, I-485 cannot be filed—monitor Visa Bulletin and maintain lawful status meanwhile.

redFlags.title: Priority date not current
redFlags.description: Client expects green card but Visa Bulletin shows years of backlog.
redFlags.fix: Monitor Visa Bulletin; maintain H-1B/status; set realistic timeline

redFlags.title: PERM recruitment issues
redFlags.description: DOL audits or inadequate recruitment advertising.
redFlags.fix: Ensure PERM compliance; experienced immigration attorney for PERM

redFlags.title: Status gap during wait
redFlags.description: Fell out of status while waiting for priority date.
redFlags.fix: Maintain valid non-immigrant status throughout wait

redFlags.title: Job change during PERM/I-140
redFlags.description: Changed employer before I-140 approved 180+ days.
redFlags.fix: Understand AC21 portability rules; may need new PERM

redFlags.title: Incorrect EB category
redFlags.description: Filed EB-3 when EB-2 qualifications exist or vice versa.

redFlags.title: Medical inadmissibility
redFlags.fix: Complete medical early; consult on waivers if needed

faqs.a: No. Multi-step process with DOL, USCIS, and visa availability. Never promise approval or timeline.
Check current Visa Bulletin—often many years backlog. Dates move monthly.
Labor certification proving no qualified US workers available—required for most EB-2/EB-3.
Depends on status—H-4 EAD, L-2, or EAD with pending I-485 if eligible.
Date PERM filed or I-140 receipt—determines place in queue per Visa Bulletin.
EB-1 for extraordinary ability/multinational managers—no PERM. EB-2 typically needs PERM unless NIW.
National Interest Waiver—EB-2 without PERM if work benefits US national interest.
I-130 through US citizen/LPR relatives—preference categories have own backlogs.

compliance: Client service agreement and consent must be on file before submission
Never guarantee visa or immigration approval; use approved-language only
Fee quotes must separate consultancy, government, and third-party costs
Green card cases typically require licensed US immigration attorney
proTips: Check Visa Bulletin monthly with client
Maintain lawful status during entire wait
PERM with experienced attorney—DOL audits real
Document AC21 eligibility if changing jobs post-I-140
Set realistic timeline expectations at sales stage
postApproval: Guide on SS card and state ID

changelog.author: Service Library
changelog.summary: Initial USA green card counselor content.

staffNotes.author: Documentation team
staffNotes.text: Never promise EB-2 timeline for India-born without showing current Visa Bulletin backlog.

quiz.question: Priority date determined by:
options: Visa Bulletin based on PERM/I-140 filing date

donts: Do not guarantee green card or timeline
Do not let status expire during wait
Do not misrepresent PERM job requirements
Do not skip priority date check
mistakes: Client fell out of status during backlog wait
Timeline promised without Visa Bulletin check
